ksa_text: Skill in developing regulatory compliance training programs tailored to different organizational roles and competency levels, including content design, delivery method selection, and effectiveness measurement.

dcwf.top_element_label: Support necessary compliance activities (e.g., ensure system security configuration guidelines are followed, compliance monitoring occurs).

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of content creation tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of content creation tools and techniques

Support necessary compliance activities (e.g., ensure system security configuration guidelines are followed, compliance monitoring occurs).

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Regulatory Compliance falls outside the primary competency scope of the BI Director. This skill KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Regulatory Compliance truly out-of-scope for the BI Director? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Regulatory Compliance as a core or recommended competency for the BI Director. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Regulatory Compliance falls outside the established competency profile for the BI Director. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Regulatory Compliance is outside the competency scope of the BI Director. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
